title: dactylorhiza comosa subsp. majalis
intro: Dactylorhiza comosa subsp. majalis, also known as the marsh helleborine, is a type of orchid native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza comosa subsp. majalis include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
